description_long: This is a rustic wooden handcart featuring a rectangular cargo bed with vertical slatted sides and two long handles for manual pulling or pushing. The cart is supported by a pair of large, spoked wooden wheels mounted on either side near the center. The cargo area contains a large barrel with metal bands and a burlap sack, suggesting it is used for transporting goods such as grain or liquids. The wood appears weathered and unfinished, giving the cart a utilitarian, historical appearance. The side rails help secure the load, while the open slat design of the platform allows for drainage and reduces weight.
description: A weathered wooden handcart with slatted sides, two large spoked wheels, and a cargo bed carrying a barrel and sack.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the cart, both handles, one wheel, and the barrel and sack in the cargo bed.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the slatted back, both side rails, and the tops of the barrel and sack.
description_view_2: Opposite side view showing the other wheel, handles, and a clear look at the barrel and sack arrangement.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the two handles, the front of the barrel and sack, and the open slatted platform.
